title: centranthus angustifolius subsp. maroccanus
intro: Centranthus angustifolius subsp. maroccanus, also known as the Moroccan centranthus or mountain bluet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centranthus angustifolius subsp. maroccanus include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
